title: Longitudinal Business Database | Stats NZ
content:
## Longitudinal Business Database
The Longitudinal Business Database (LBD) is a large research database. It holds de-identified microdata about businesses.
Data comes from a range of Stats NZ surveys and government agencies. The data is linked together, or integrated, to form the LBD.
The LBD complements the Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI), which contains de-identified microdata about people and households. The two databases are linked through tax data.
Researchers use the LBD to evaluate policies and analyse business performance.
How the LBD is being used Data in the LBD

## How the LBD is being used
Researchers use the LBD to answer questions about business, industries, employment, and innovation.
This research can inform work on complex issues, such as understanding what makes firms productive.
Research using Stats NZ microdata is a tool for browsing all research projects that have used the IDI and LBD and their published findings.
The LBD has data on the following topics:
- International trade and tourism.
The LBD is a growing source of information. We update the data annually, and often add new datasets.
Data in the LBD has detailed descriptions of data available for research projects.
Page updated 23 August 2022
